# feat(nexus): composable HTTP middleware injection via @app.use_middleware (#449)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `1be8b5ea5ac4` — feat(nexus): composable HTTP middleware injection via @app.use_middleware (#449)

**Body**:

Adds `Nexus.use_middleware` so downstream apps can register function-style
HTTP middleware with a single decorator — the shape developers already
reach for when writing observability, auth header injection, and per-request
hooks — instead of hand-rolling a BaseHTTPMiddleware subclass for every
one-liner.

The decorator name (`use_middleware` rather than plain `middleware`)
sidesteps collision with the existing `middleware` property that returns
the registered-middleware list for introspection. The decorated function
is wrapped in a per-function BaseHTTPMiddleware subclass so the existing
duplicate-detection and ordering logic in `add_middleware` continues to
work — two decorated functions produce two distinct wrapper classes,
each with a name that references the original function for log-grep.

Validation rejects non-callables, classes, and sync functions with typed
TypeError messages. Sync rejection matters because
BaseHTTPMiddleware.dispatch must return an awaitable; a sync function
would surface as a cryptic "coroutine expected" at request time rather
than at registration.

Tests cover: async happy path, sync rejection, non-callable rejection,
class rejection, original-function return, wrapper naming, distinct
wrappers per function (Tier 1), plus a Tier 2 integration test that
verifies the middleware actually runs in the request path through the
real gateway — addresses the orphan-detection invariant that a facade
without a production call site is a lie.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
